mandate.title: HR Onboarding Automation System
mandate.description: A web-based application to digitize and automate the employee onboarding process for a 50-person French company. The system will replace manual paper-based workflows with automated document generation, e-signature tracking, email notifications, and progress monitoring, reducing onboarding time from 2 weeks to under 3 days.
target_users: HR Manager (primary user who initiates and manages onboarding workflows)
New employees (who complete forms and sign documents digitally)
Office/IT administrator (who may need to provision accounts or access reports)
core_features: Digital onboarding workflow builder and tracker
Document generation from templates (employment contracts, policies, forms)
Progress dashboard showing status of all active onboardings
success_criteria: Reduce average onboarding completion time from 14 days to 3 days or less
Reduce HR manager time spent on onboarding tasks by 70%
Zero lost documents or missed signatures during onboarding
System adoption by HR team within 2 weeks of launch
constraints: Must integrate with existing Google Workspace environment
Must comply with French labor law and GDPR requirements
Small team (50 employees, ~10 hires/year) - low transaction volume
Decision maker is HR Manager, not technical stakeholder
Timeline pressure unknown - needs clarification during discovery
team_needs: Full-stack web developer (React/Vue + Node.js or Python backend)
French HR compliance consultant (for document templates and legal requirements)
QA engineer for workflow and integration testing

tensions.wants: Define clear boundaries for the HR onboarding automation system
tensions.value: Core: Digital onboarding workflow (document generation, e-signature tracking, email automation, progress dashboard). Integration with Google Workspace. OUT OF SCOPE: Payroll integration, full HRIS, performance management, recruitment/ATS features

doubts.about: Whether client needs multi-language support (France-based but brief in English)
Specific document types and templates required
Whether they need mobile access or web-only is sufficient
Compliance requirements for French labor law (GDPR, employee data retention)

tensions.wants: Identify who will use this system daily
value: HR Manager (primary user - initiates onboarding, tracks progress)

doubts.about: Whether department managers need access to track their new hires
Whether C-level executives need reporting/oversight dashboards

tensions.wants: Define measurable outcomes
value: Reduce onboarding time from 2 weeks to <3 days
Eliminate manual email sending for onboarding
HR manager reports time savings of >70%
Zero lost documents or missed signatures

doubts.about: Client's actual tolerance for onboarding duration - is 3 days acceptable or do they want faster?
Whether they need audit trail for compliance purposes

tensions.wants: Identify required technical skills
value: UI/UX designer familiar with internal tools

doubts.about: Whether we need a dedicated DevOps engineer or if deployment can be handled by full-stack dev
Level of custom document generation complexity - might need specialist

tensions.wants: Provide realistic delivery estimate
tensions.value: 12-16 weeks (3-4 months): 2 weeks discovery/design, 8-10 weeks development, 2-3 weeks testing/deployment, 1 week training/handover

doubts.about: Whether client needs this urgently or timeline is flexible
Availability of client stakeholders for requirements gathering and UAT
Complexity of French compliance requirements may extend timeline

tensions.wants: Assess if budget aligns with scope
tensions.value: €15k-€30k is tight but feasible for MVP. Estimate €22k-€28k for core features with basic UI. Will require scope discipline and potentially phased delivery

doubts.about: Whether compliance consultation costs fit within budget
E-signature API costs (per-document fees) - ongoing operational cost not clear who pays
Whether client expects ongoing maintenance/support within this budget

tensions.wants: Recommend appropriate technology choices
tensions.value: Web app (React + Node.js), Google Workspace APIs, E-signature API (DocuSign/PandaDoc), PostgreSQL, hosted on cloud (GCP for Google integration or AWS)

doubts.about: Whether to build custom or use low-code platform (Retool/n8n) to meet budget
French data residency requirements may dictate hosting location

tensions.wants: Clarify what needs to connect with existing systems
tensions.value: Google Workspace (Gmail for email automation, Drive for document storage, potentially Calendar for onboarding schedules). E-signature service API. Export to Excel for legacy compatibility

doubts.about: Whether they use any existing HR software that needs integration
Authentication method - Google SSO assumed but not confirmed

tensions.wants: Identify regulatory and legal considerations
tensions.value: GDPR compliance mandatory (France-based, employee data). French labor law document requirements. Data retention policies. Right to access/deletion

doubts.about: Specific French HR document requirements and formats
Whether works council (comité social et économique) needs to approve system
Cross-border considerations if they hire remote workers outside France

tensions.wants: Assess future growth needs
tensions.value: Current: 50 employees, 10 hires/year. System should handle 100 employees, 25 hires/year without re-architecture. Low volume means performance is not a concern

doubts.about: Company growth plans - are they expecting rapid expansion?
Whether they plan to expand to other HR processes later (offboarding, training tracking)

tech_stack.rationale: Next.js provides SSR/SSG capabilities for fast page loads and SEO, TypeScript ensures type safety for complex HR workflows, Express offers flexibility for API design, PostgreSQL provides ACID compliance for employee data integrity. React-hook-form and zod handle form validation, Prisma simplifies database access, nodemailer enables email automation, pdf-lib generates employment documents, BullMQ handles async tasks like scheduled reminders. This stack is independent of unresolved compliance and budget constraints - French data residency affects hosting choice, not core technology. Integration requirements may require additional libraries but these provide the foundation.

deployment_model.hosting: Deferred - blocked by compliance_risks and budget_fit
deployment_model.approach: Docker containerization with GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline, environment-based configuration for dev/staging/production
deployment_model.rationale: Containerization ensures consistent deployment across environments regardless of final hosting choice. CI/CD automation reduces deployment errors and enables rapid iteration. Hosting provider selection genuinely requires compliance clarity (French data residency, GDPR) and budget allocation (EU hosting premium vs US hosting). Deployment approach itself is sound and can be decided independently - it works equally well on AWS EU, Azure France, Scaleway, or on-premise infrastructure.

vendors.recommendation: Sentry (error tracking), stdout logging captured by hosting provider
vendors.rationale: Sentry provides excellent error tracking with EU data residency support, free tier sufficient for expected scale (~50 employees onboarding). Stdout logging keeps infrastructure simple and portable across hosting providers. Audit logging depth remains unclear - may need dedicated solution if compliance requires comprehensive action logs, but basic monitoring can proceed with this stack.

vendors.recommendation: SendGrid or Postmark (both support EU regions), fallback to client SMTP relay
vendors.rationale: Both vendors support EU data residency and have reasonable pricing for transactional email at this scale. SendGrid more feature-rich, Postmark simpler and focused on deliverability. If budget is constrained, using client's existing SMTP relay costs nothing but requires their infrastructure details. Cannot finalize until budget_fit resolved, but shortlist is defensible.

vendors.recommendation: Deferred - cannot shortlist without compliance guidance
vendors.rationale: French labor law may require eIDAS-qualified electronic signatures for employment contracts. DocuSign, Adobe Sign, and Yousign all operate in France, but certification requirements and per-document costs vary dramatically. Yousign specializes in French market and may be most cost-effective, but without knowing specific document types (CDI, CDD, amendments, NDAs) and budget allocation for per-signature costs, recommendation would be speculation. This decision is genuinely blocked.

deferred.decision: Hosting provider selection (AWS EU, Azure France Central, Scaleway, OVH, or on-premise)
deferred.blocked_by: compliance_risks (French data residency requirements, GDPR considerations) and budget_fit (EU hosting costs premium over US regions)

deferred.decision: E-signature vendor (DocuSign, Adobe Sign, Yousign, Universign)
deferred.blocked_by: compliance_risks (eIDAS certification requirements unknown) and budget_fit (per-document costs unallocated) and project_scope (document types requiring signatures unspecified)

deferred.decision: Document storage architecture (S3-compatible object storage vs database blobs vs filesystem)
deferred.blocked_by: compliance_risks (data retention policies undefined, affects storage design and costs) and budget_fit (object storage costs vs database scaling)

deferred.decision: Audit logging depth and retention (basic application logs vs comprehensive action audit trail)
deferred.blocked_by: compliance_risks (GDPR and French labor law may require detailed audit logs with specific retention periods)

deferred.decision: Authentication provider (Google OAuth assumed but unconfirmed)
deferred.blocked_by: integration_requirements (client's existing identity provider unknown, may use Microsoft 365, Okta, or custom LDAP)

deferred.decision: Final email vendor (SendGrid vs Postmark vs client SMTP)
deferred.blocked_by: budget_fit (unclear if transactional email service budget exists or must use free client SMTP)

blueprint.summary: We are building an HR onboarding automation system for a France-based client (<50 employees) to reduce onboarding time from 2 weeks to <3 days and eliminate manual email/document tracking. The CTO has flagged critical blockers: compliance requirements (GDPR, French labor law, e-signature certification) and budget constraints prevent selection of hosting providers, e-signature vendors, and email services. Core stack assumptions (React, Node.js, PostgreSQL) exist but lack confidence due to unresolved dependencies. The architecture cannot be designed honestly until compliance, budget, document requirements, and authentication method are clarified.

components.name: Frontend Application
components.responsibility: React-based SPA for HR managers to create/manage onboarding workflows, track employee progress, and generate documents. Assumed responsive web-only unless mobile requirement emerges.

components.name: Backend API
components.responsibility: Node.js/Express REST API handling authentication, workflow orchestration, document generation, and integration with external services (email, e-signature).

components.name: PostgreSQL Database
components.responsibility: Primary data store for users, employees, workflow definitions, workflow instances, documents metadata, and audit logs. GDPR compliance requirements will dictate encryption and retention policies.

components.name: Document Storage
components.responsibility: Storage for generated and uploaded documents. Recommendation is S3-compatible object storage (MinIO or EU provider like Scaleway) but blocked by budget and data residency requirements.

components.name: Document Generator
components.responsibility: Service/module for generating PDFs from templates using pdf-lib or similar. May need more sophisticated tooling depending on template complexity.

components.name: Email Service Integration
components.responsibility: Adapter for transactional email (SendGrid/Postmark/SMTP). Sends onboarding notifications, reminders, and status updates. Vendor selection blocked by budget clarity.

components.name: E-signature Integration
components.responsibility: Adapter for e-signature provider (DocuSign, Yousign, or eIDAS-certified alternative). Critical path blocked by French labor law compliance requirements and budget.

components.name: Authentication Service
components.responsibility: OAuth2 integration for user authentication. Google SSO assumed but unconfirmed - may need Microsoft, Okta, or custom LDAP.

components.name: Job Queue
components.responsibility: Optional async task processor (BullMQ or similar) for document generation, email sending, and scheduled reminders. Necessity depends on task complexity and volume.

risks.description: French labor law requirements for e-signatures, document types, and GDPR employee data handling are undefined. This blocks selection of e-signature vendor (may require eIDAS certification), data retention policies, and audit logging depth. Works council approval may be required.
risks.mitigation: Client must provide compliance guidance or engage French HR legal consultant during discovery phase. Budget 1-2 weeks for compliance research before technical design.

risks.description: Budget allocation unclear for ongoing operational costs (e-signature per-document fees, email service, EU-based hosting, monitoring tools). CTO correctly flagged this prevents honest vendor selection.
risks.mitigation: Client must clarify total budget, operational cost ownership, and maintenance expectations. Consider cost-optimized alternatives (self-hosted email via SMTP, MinIO for storage, free tiers where viable).

risks.description: Document types, templates, workflow variations, and user roles not specified. Cannot design data model, API contracts, or workflow engine without this clarity.
risks.mitigation: Discovery phase must produce: list of document templates, workflow step definitions, role matrix (HR admin, manager, exec), and integration requirements for existing systems.

risks.description: Google SSO assumed throughout but unconfirmed. Wrong assumption could require significant rework of auth integration.
risks.mitigation: Confirm identity provider in discovery kickoff. Design OAuth2 adapter interface to allow provider swapping if needed.

risks.description: pdf-lib may be insufficient for complex French HR document templates (multi-page contracts, dynamic tables, legal formatting requirements). May need more sophisticated tooling (Puppeteer, LaTeX, or commercial PDF generation service).
risks.mitigation: Review sample document templates during discovery. If complexity is high, budget for document generation specialist or commercial tool (e.g., DocRaptor, PDFMonkey).

risks.description: 12-16 week timeline assumes straightforward requirements. French compliance research and potential works council approval could extend timeline significantly.
risks.mitigation: Front-load compliance research in discovery phase. Set client expectation that timeline may extend to 18-20 weeks if compliance complexity emerges.

risks.description: EU data residency requirements likely given French context, but specific requirements undefined. EU-based hosting (AWS eu-west-3, Scaleway, OVH) typically costs 20-30% more than US regions.
risks.mitigation: Clarify data residency requirements explicitly. Budget for EU hosting if confirmed. Consider Scaleway (French provider) for cost-effective EU compliance.

risks.description: E-signature integration will likely become a critical dependency. Wrong provider choice (e.g., selecting DocuSign if eIDAS certification required) could necessitate costly migration.
risks.mitigation: Design e-signature adapter interface to abstract provider. Confirm eIDAS requirements before vendor commitment. Consider Yousign (French provider with eIDAS) as safer choice for French market.

risks.description: Architecture assumes <50 employee org with moderate growth. If client has aggressive expansion plans or plans to white-label solution, current monolith approach may be inadequate.
risks.mitigation: Clarify growth projections in discovery. Current monolith is appropriate for stated scale, but document architectural migration path if scale exceeds 500 employees or multi-tenant requirements emerge.

blockers.blocked_by: compliance_risks (0.1 confidence) - data retention policies, GDPR requirements, and audit logging depth undefined; project_scope (0.1 confidence) - document types and required fields unknown
arch_data_model (0.2 confidence) - cannot define request/response shapes without entity design; cto_security_model (0.1 confidence) - authentication method unconfirmed
cto_vendor_esignature (0.1 confidence) - vendor selection blocked by French labor law compliance uncertainty and budget constraints

cto_deployment_model (0.1 confidence) - hosting provider unclear due to data residency and budget; cto_architecture_pattern (0.1 confidence) - storage approach (filesystem vs object storage) undefined
cto_vendor_email (0.1 confidence) - vendor selection blocked by budget uncertainty; may need to use client's SMTP instead
integration_requirements (0.1 confidence) - authentication method (Google SSO vs other) unconfirmed; cto_security_model (0.1 confidence) - OAuth2 specifics undefined
project_scope (0.1 confidence) - workflow complexity and variations unknown; target_users (0.1 confidence) - whether managers need workflow customization unclear
compliance_risks (0.1 confidence) - French compliance may require comprehensive action logs; success_criteria (0.7 confidence) notes audit trail uncertainty

audit.verdict_rationale: The pipeline contains blocking inconsistencies where unresolved compliance and budget tensions prevent CTO and Architect from making technical decisions, rendering the Field unreliable for client presentation without revision.

inconsistencies.description: CTO cannot select e-signature vendor due to unresolved compliance requirements, creating a fundamental gap in the technical design.
Budget uncertainties block deployment model choices, contradicting the partial timeline estimate that assumes budget alignment.
Architect cannot design data model because project scope details like document types are unresolved, despite CEO's active scope tension.

false_blockers.reason: Sentry's free tier can be used as a default without budget clarity, making the budget doubt a low-severity preference rather than a blocking decision.

scope_reality_check.assessment: The scope involves complex French compliance, integrations, and ongoing operational costs, but with an unknown budget, it appears potentially underestimated for a small organization.

discovery_questions.question: What is the total budget you have allocated for this project, including development costs and any ongoing fees like e-signature or hosting?
What specific French labor laws or GDPR requirements must the system meet, such as data retention policies or document formats?
What types of documents will be generated and signed during the onboarding process?
Do you currently use any HR software or systems that the new onboarding tool needs to connect with?
Who will primarily use this system, and do department managers or executives need special access features?
How quickly do you need this system delivered, and how available will your team be for testing and feedback?
Do you expect the system to handle future growth, like expanding to other HR processes or more employees?
Will the system need to support multiple languages or mobile access?

qa.reasoning_raw:
## Preparing Field Analysis  
- The process starts by calling read_field to gather comprehensive information about the field.  
- This initial step involves building a mental map to track each agent's actions and contributions.  

## Generating Audit Tensions  
- After analyzing the field, use update_field with qa_ prefixes to document 3-5 audit tensions.  
- These tensions will highlight key areas of concern or discrepancies in the field.  

## Producing Final Output  
- Follow the exact format for function calls to ensure consistency.  
- The final step is to produce an AuditReport schema based on the gathered and analyzed data.

---

## Mapping key perspectives  
- The CEO focuses on project scope, target users, partial success criteria, team needs, estimated timeline, budget fit, tech stack, integration, compliance, and scaling.  
- The CTO emphasizes tech stack, deployment, vendors like email and e-signature, security, architecture, and challenges compliance and budget as blockers.  
- The Architect works on partial data model, API design, component boundaries, integration strategy, document storage, and workflow engine, but is blocked by unresolved issues.  

## Identifying tensions  
- Unresolved compliance and budget issues block technical decisions, making the pipeline incoherent with high confidence (0.9).  
- The project scope seems ambitious given unclear budget signals, raising feasibility concerns.  
- Compliance doubts may be overstated, potentially overblocking progress, while budget uncertainty is a real, not false, blocker.  

## Assessing project health  
- The project likely faces a red verdict due to blocking inconsistencies, like conflicts between CEO scope and CTO decisions.  
- Possible false blockers exist, such as deferred budget decisions, but scope seems overestimated with unknown budget details.  

## Proposing discovery questions  
- Prioritize questions on compliance risks, budget clarity, and project scope details to unblock tensions.  
- Limit to 8 critical, non-technical questions, ordered by priority, each linked to specific tension IDs for resolution.

---

## Reviewing Audit Report  
- The audit resulted in a "red" verdict due to blocking inconsistencies, rendering the field unreliable for client presentation.  
- Key issues include fundamental contradictions, such as CTO and Architect roles being stalled without client input on compliance and budget.  

## Identifying Inconsistencies  
- Major inconsistencies include compliance_risks (active, low confidence) conflicting with cto_vendor_esignature, marked as a blocking severity issue.  
- Another conflict is between budget_fit (active, low) and cto_deployment_model, contributing to the overall unreliability.  

## Assessing Scope and Budget  
- The scope involves complex compliance and integrations, but the budget remains unknown, suggesting possible underestimation.  
- Confidence in budget_vs_scope is low at 0.5, highlighting significant uncertainty in project planning.  

## Prioritizing Discovery Questions  
- Need to create up to 8 prioritized questions, starting with critical ones on compliance, budget, and project scope basics.  
- Examples include asking for the total project budget, specific French labor law requirements, and documents for onboarding, each designed to resolve identified tensions.

report.internal:
# HR Onboarding Automation System

> A web-based application to digitize and automate the employee onboarding process for a 50-person French company. The system will replace manual paper-based workflows with automated document generation, e-signature tracking, email notifications, and progress monitoring, reducing onboarding time from 2 weeks to under 3 days.

**Project ID:** `proj-Fi5bPsCI` · **Pipeline:** 321.8s · **Tokens:** 112 843 · **Field confidence:** 12%

---

## ✕ RED

The pipeline contains blocking inconsistencies where unresolved compliance and budget tensions prevent CTO and Architect from making technical decisions, rendering the Field unreliable for client presentation without revision.

## Mandate

**Complexity:** medium

**Target users**
- HR Manager (primary user who initiates and manages onboarding workflows)
- New employees (who complete forms and sign documents digitally)
- Office/IT administrator (who may need to provision accounts or access reports)

**Core features**
- Digital onboarding workflow builder and tracker
- Automated email notifications and reminders
- Document generation from templates (employment contracts, policies, forms)
- E-signature integration and tracking
- Google Workspace integration (Gmail, Drive, SSO)
- Progress dashboard showing status of all active onboardings
- Document storage and retrieval system
- Basic reporting on onboarding metrics

**Success criteria**
- Reduce average onboarding completion time from 14 days to 3 days or less
- Eliminate 100% of paper-based onboarding documents
- Reduce HR manager time spent on onboarding tasks by 70%
- Zero lost documents or missed signatures during onboarding
- System adoption by HR team within 2 weeks of launch

**Constraints**
- Budget: €15,000-€30,000 (estimated €22k-€28k for MVP)
- Must integrate with existing Google Workspace environment
- Must comply with French labor law and GDPR requirements
- Small team (50 employees, ~10 hires/year) - low transaction volume
- Decision maker is HR Manager, not technical stakeholder
- Timeline pressure unknown - needs clarification during discovery

**Team needs**
- Full-stack web developer (React/Vue + Node.js or Python backend)
- Google Workspace API integration specialist
- E-signature service integration developer
- UI/UX designer for internal tooling
- French HR compliance consultant (for document templates and legal requirements)
- QA engineer for workflow and integration testing
- Project manager/liaison for client communication

## Technical Decisions

### Stack

| Layer | Choice |
|-------|--------|
| Frontend | Next.js 14 with TypeScript |
| Backend | Node.js with Express |
| Database | PostgreSQL |
| Key libs | react-hook-form, zod, prisma, nodemailer, pdf-lib, BullMQ |

*Confidence: 75% — Next.js provides SSR/SSG capabilities for fast page loads and SEO, TypeScript ensures type safety for complex HR workflows, Express offers flexibility for API design, PostgreSQL provides ACID compliance for employee data integrity. React-hook-form and zod handle form validation, Prisma simplifies database access, nodemailer enables email automation, pdf-lib generates employment documents, BullMQ handles async tasks like scheduled reminders. This stack is independent of unresolved compliance and budget constraints - French data residency affects hosting choice, not core technology. Integration requirements may require additional libraries but these provide the foundation.*

### Deployment

**Hosting:** Deferred - blocked by compliance_risks and budget_fit · **Approach:** Docker containerization with GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline, environment-based configuration for dev/staging/production

*Confidence: 65% — Containerization ensures consistent deployment across environments regardless of final hosting choice. CI/CD automation reduces deployment errors and enables rapid iteration. Hosting provider selection genuinely requires compliance clarity (French data residency, GDPR) and budget allocation (EU hosting premium vs US hosting). Deployment approach itself is sound and can be decided independently - it works equally well on AWS EU, Azure France, Scaleway, or on-premise infrastructure.*

### Vendors

| Category | Recommendation | Decision | Confidence |
|----------|---------------|----------|------------|
| monitoring | Sentry (error tracking), stdout logging captured by hosting provider | shortlisted | 70% |
| email | SendGrid or Postmark (both support EU regions), fallback to client SMTP relay | shortlisted | 65% |
| e-signature | Deferred - cannot shortlist without compliance guidance | deferred | 10% |

### Deferred decisions

- **Hosting provider selection (AWS EU, Azure France Central, Scaleway, OVH, or on-premise)** — blocked by `compliance_risks (French data residency requirements, GDPR considerations) and budget_fit (EU hosting costs premium over US regions)`
- **E-signature vendor (DocuSign, Adobe Sign, Yousign, Universign)** — blocked by `compliance_risks (eIDAS certification requirements unknown) and budget_fit (per-document costs unallocated) and project_scope (document types requiring signatures unspecified)`
- **Document storage architecture (S3-compatible object storage vs database blobs vs filesystem)** — blocked by `compliance_risks (data retention policies undefined, affects storage design and costs) and budget_fit (object storage costs vs database scaling)`
- **Audit logging depth and retention (basic application logs vs comprehensive action audit trail)** — blocked by `compliance_risks (GDPR and French labor law may require detailed audit logs with specific retention periods)`
- **Authentication provider (Google OAuth assumed but unconfirmed)** — blocked by `integration_requirements (client's existing identity provider unknown, may use Microsoft 365, Okta, or custom LDAP)`
- **Final email vendor (SendGrid vs Postmark vs client SMTP)** — blocked by `budget_fit (unclear if transactional email service budget exists or must use free client SMTP)`

## Architecture

We are building an HR onboarding automation system for a France-based client (<50 employees) to reduce onboarding time from 2 weeks to <3 days and eliminate manual email/document tracking. The CTO has flagged critical blockers: compliance requirements (GDPR, French labor law, e-signature certification) and budget constraints prevent selection of hosting providers, e-signature vendors, and email services. Core stack assumptions (React, Node.js, PostgreSQL) exist but lack confidence due to unresolved dependencies. The architecture cannot be designed honestly until compliance, budget, document requirements, and authentication method are clarified.

### Components

| Name | Type | Responsibility | Confidence |
|------|------|----------------|------------|
| PostgreSQL Database | database | Primary data store for users, employees, workflow definitions, workflow instances, documents metadata, and audit logs. GDPR compliance requirements will dictate encryption and retention policies. | 60% |

### Architecture blockers

- **Data Model Design** — blocked by `compliance_risks (0.1 confidence) - data retention policies, GDPR requirements, and audit logging depth undefined; project_scope (0.1 confidence) - document types and required fields unknown`
- **API Contract Specification** — blocked by `arch_data_model (0.2 confidence) - cannot define request/response shapes without entity design; cto_security_model (0.1 confidence) - authentication method unconfirmed`
- **E-signature Integration Architecture** — blocked by `cto_vendor_esignature (0.1 confidence) - vendor selection blocked by French labor law compliance uncertainty and budget constraints`
- **Document Storage Implementation** — blocked by `cto_deployment_model (0.1 confidence) - hosting provider unclear due to data residency and budget; cto_architecture_pattern (0.1 confidence) - storage approach (filesystem vs object storage) undefined`
- **Email Integration Implementation** — blocked by `cto_vendor_email (0.1 confidence) - vendor selection blocked by budget uncertainty; may need to use client's SMTP instead`
- **Authentication Flow Design** — blocked by `integration_requirements (0.1 confidence) - authentication method (Google SSO vs other) unconfirmed; cto_security_model (0.1 confidence) - OAuth2 specifics undefined`
- **Workflow Engine Design** — blocked by `project_scope (0.1 confidence) - workflow complexity and variations unknown; target_users (0.1 confidence) - whether managers need workflow customization unclear`
- **Audit Logging Depth** — blocked by `compliance_risks (0.1 confidence) - French compliance may require comprehensive action logs; success_criteria (0.7 confidence) notes audit trail uncertainty`

## Risks

| Severity | Area | Description | Mitigation |
|----------|------|-------------|------------|
| BLOCKING | Compliance and Legal | French labor law requirements for e-signatures, document types, and GDPR employee data handling are undefined. This blocks selection of e-signature vendor (may require eIDAS certification), data retention policies, and audit logging depth. Works council approval may be required. | Client must provide compliance guidance or engage French HR legal consultant during discovery phase. Budget 1-2 weeks for compliance research before technical design. |
| BLOCKING | Budget Constraints | Budget allocation unclear for ongoing operational costs (e-signature per-document fees, email service, EU-based hosting, monitoring tools). CTO correctly flagged this prevents honest vendor selection. | Client must clarify total budget, operational cost ownership, and maintenance expectations. Consider cost-optimized alternatives (self-hosted email via SMTP, MinIO for storage, free tiers where viable). |
| BLOCKING | Scope Ambiguity | Document types, templates, workflow variations, and user roles not specified. Cannot design data model, API contracts, or workflow engine without this clarity. | Discovery phase must produce: list of document templates, workflow step definitions, role matrix (HR admin, manager, exec), and integration requirements for existing systems. |
| HIGH | E-signature Provider Lock-in | E-signature integration will likely become a critical dependency. Wrong provider choice (e.g., selecting DocuSign if eIDAS certification required) could necessitate costly migration. | Design e-signature adapter interface to abstract provider. Confirm eIDAS requirements before vendor commitment. Consider Yousign (French provider with eIDAS) as safer choice for French market. |
| MEDIUM | Authentication Provider | Google SSO assumed throughout but unconfirmed. Wrong assumption could require significant rework of auth integration. | Confirm identity provider in discovery kickoff. Design OAuth2 adapter interface to allow provider swapping if needed. |
| MEDIUM | Document Generation Complexity | pdf-lib may be insufficient for complex French HR document templates (multi-page contracts, dynamic tables, legal formatting requirements). May need more sophisticated tooling (Puppeteer, LaTeX, or commercial PDF generation service). | Review sample document templates during discovery. If complexity is high, budget for document generation specialist or commercial tool (e.g., DocRaptor, PDFMonkey). |
| MEDIUM | Timeline Pressure vs. Compliance | 12-16 week timeline assumes straightforward requirements. French compliance research and potential works council approval could extend timeline significantly. | Front-load compliance research in discovery phase. Set client expectation that timeline may extend to 18-20 weeks if compliance complexity emerges. |
| MEDIUM | Data Residency and Hosting Costs | EU data residency requirements likely given French context, but specific requirements undefined. EU-based hosting (AWS eu-west-3, Scaleway, OVH) typically costs 20-30% more than US regions. | Clarify data residency requirements explicitly. Budget for EU hosting if confirmed. Consider Scaleway (French provider) for cost-effective EU compliance. |
| LOW | Scalability Assumptions | Architecture assumes <50 employee org with moderate growth. If client has aggressive expansion plans or plans to white-label solution, current monolith approach may be inadequate. | Clarify growth projections in discovery. Current monolith is appropriate for stated scale, but document architectural migration path if scale exceeds 500 employees or multi-tenant requirements emerge. |

## Discovery Questions

*Questions to ask the client before proceeding.*

1. **[CRITICAL]** What is the total budget you have allocated for this project, including development costs and any ongoing fees like e-signature or hosting?
   *Unblocks: budget_fit, cto_deployment_model, cto_vendor_esignature*
2. **[CRITICAL]** What specific French labor laws or GDPR requirements must the system meet, such as data retention policies or document formats?
   *Unblocks: compliance_risks, cto_security_model, arch_data_model*
3. **[CRITICAL]** What types of documents will be generated and signed during the onboarding process?
   *Unblocks: project_scope, cto_vendor_esignature, arch_data_model*
4. **[HIGH]** Do you currently use any HR software or systems that the new onboarding tool needs to connect with?
   *Unblocks: integration_requirements, arch_integration_strategy*
5. **[HIGH]** Who will primarily use this system, and do department managers or executives need special access features?
   *Unblocks: target_users, cto_security_model*
6. **[MEDIUM]** How quickly do you need this system delivered, and how available will your team be for testing and feedback?
   *Unblocks: estimated_timeline, success_criteria*
7. **[MEDIUM]** Do you expect the system to handle future growth, like expanding to other HR processes or more employees?
   *Unblocks: scaling_considerations, cto_architecture_pattern*
8. **[MEDIUM]** Will the system need to support multiple languages or mobile access?
   *Unblocks: project_scope*

## Inconsistencies

- **[BLOCKING]** CTO cannot select e-signature vendor due to unresolved compliance requirements, creating a fundamental gap in the technical design.
  *Between: compliance_risks ↔ cto_vendor_esignature*

## False Blockers

## Scope Reality Check

report.client:
# HR Onboarding Automation System

> A web-based application to digitize and automate the employee onboarding process for a 50-person French company. The system will replace manual paper-based workflows with automated document generation, e-signature tracking, email notifications, and progress monitoring, reducing onboarding time from 2 weeks to under 3 days.

## 🔴 Needs Clarification

The pipeline contains blocking inconsistencies where unresolved compliance and budget tensions prevent CTO and Architect from making technical decisions, rendering the Field unreliable for client presentation without revision.

## What We Understood

**Who will use this**
- HR Manager (primary user who initiates and manages onboarding workflows)
- New employees (who complete forms and sign documents digitally)
- Office/IT administrator (who may need to provision accounts or access reports)

**What we're building**
- Digital onboarding workflow builder and tracker
- Automated email notifications and reminders
- Document generation from templates (employment contracts, policies, forms)
- E-signature integration and tracking
- Google Workspace integration (Gmail, Drive, SSO)
- Progress dashboard showing status of all active onboardings
- Document storage and retrieval system
- Basic reporting on onboarding metrics

**Constraints we're working within**
- Budget: €15,000-€30,000 (estimated €22k-€28k for MVP)
- Must integrate with existing Google Workspace environment
- Must comply with French labor law and GDPR requirements
- Small team (50 employees, ~10 hires/year) - low transaction volume
- Decision maker is HR Manager, not technical stakeholder
- Timeline pressure unknown - needs clarification during discovery

## What We've Decided

- Built with Next.js 14 with TypeScript and Node.js with Express, using PostgreSQL as database
- Hosted on Deferred - blocked by compliance_risks and budget_fit — Docker containerization with GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline, environment-based configuration for dev/staging/production
- Considering Sentry (error tracking), stdout logging captured by hosting provider for monitoring
- Considering SendGrid or Postmark (both support EU regions), fallback to client SMTP relay for email

## Questions for Our Discovery Call

1. What is the total budget you have allocated for this project, including development costs and any ongoing fees like e-signature or hosting?
2. What specific French labor laws or GDPR requirements must the system meet, such as data retention policies or document formats?
3. What types of documents will be generated and signed during the onboarding process?
4. Do you currently use any HR software or systems that the new onboarding tool needs to connect with?
5. Who will primarily use this system, and do department managers or executives need special access features?
6. How quickly do you need this system delivered, and how available will your team be for testing and feedback?
7. Do you expect the system to handle future growth, like expanding to other HR processes or more employees?
8. Will the system need to support multiple languages or mobile access?

## Our Assessment

The scope involves complex French compliance, integrations, and ongoing operational costs, but with an unknown budget, it appears potentially underestimated for a small organization.

We need budget confirmation before assessing scope fit

---
*Freed Agents · 2026-03-12*
